01 — About

About — Todd Libby

1

Introduction

Former web developer and accessibility advocate based in Phoenix, Arizona. I used to build things for the web that worked for everyone — not just most people. Now I just putt around and write about it.

I had been working on the web for over two decades, writing HTML, CSS, and JavaScript long before frameworks were a thing. These days my stack is HTML and vanilla CSS. Nuxt, TypeScript, and Node — because I still believe the platform is powerful enough on its own, but sometimes, it needs help. I am also a big fan of Nuxt.

Accessibility is a right. Not a privilege. It's how I think from the concept phase to the first line of markup and up until the finished product. Accessibility, semantic HTML, and progressive enhancement are non-negotiable starting points.

"The web is for everyone make sure it is accessible."

When I'm not streaming DDO on Twitch, tending to some coding project, I am writing about things at toddl.dev.
